response:
Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `is_valid_email` function using pytest style, covering happy path, edge cases, and error cases:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_valid_email  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

def test_valid_emails():
    """Test valid email addresses (happy path)"""
    valid_emails = [
        "test@example.com",
        "user.name+tag@example.co.uk",
        "first.last@sub.domain.org",
        "12345@test.com",
        "user_name@domain-name.com",
        "user%name@domain.com",
        "user-name@domain.com",
        "user.name@domain.com",
        "user@sub.domain.co.uk",
        "user@123.123.123.123"  # Valid according to the regex (though not all email systems accept IP addresses)
    ]
    for email in valid_emails:
        assert is_valid_email(email) is True

def test_invalid_emails():
    """Test invalid email addresses"""
    invalid_emails = [
        # Missing @
        "testexample.com",
        # Missing domain
        "test@",
        # Missing TLD
        "test@example",
        # Invalid characters
        "test@exa mple.com",
        "test@example..com",
        "test@example.com.",
        ".test@example.com",
        "test@example_com",
        "test@example#.com",
        # No local part
        "@example.com",
        # Consecutive dots
        "test..user@example.com",
        # TLD too short
        "test@example.c",
        # TLD too long (if we consider the regex allows any length, but typically TLDs are 2-6 chars)
        "test@example.abcdefgh",
        # No TLD
        "test@example.",
        # IP address with invalid format
        "user@123.123.123",
        "user@123.123.123.123.123",
        # Unicode characters (not allowed by this regex)
        "üñîçøðé@example.com",
        "test@exämple.com",
        # Spaces
        " test@example.com",
        "test@example.com ",
        "test example@example.com",
        # Empty string
        "",
        # None (though type hint suggests str, we might want to test this)
        None
    ]
    for email in invalid_emails:
        if email is None:
            with pytest.raises(TypeError):
                is_valid_email(email)
        else:
            assert is_valid_email(email) is False

def test_edge_cases():
    """Test edge cases"""
    # Minimum valid length
    assert is_valid_email("a@b.co") is True

    # Maximum reasonable length (though regex doesn't enforce max length)
    long_email = "a" * 64 + "@" + "b" * 63 + "." + "com"
    assert is_valid_email(long_email) is True

    # Email with all allowed special characters
    assert is_valid_email("a.b+c-d_e%g@h-i.j.k-l.m.co") is True

    # Email with numbers only
    assert is_valid_email("1234567890@1234567890.com") is True

    # Subdomains
    assert is_valid_email("test@a.b.c.d.e.f.co.uk") is True

    # Internationalized domain names (IDN) - not supported by this regex
    assert is_valid_email("test@例子.测试") is False

def test_case_insensitivity():
    """Test that email validation is case insensitive for the local part"""
    assert is_valid_email("Test.User@Example.COM") is True
    assert is_valid_email("TEST.USER@EXAMPLE.COM") is True
    assert is_valid_email("test.user@example.com") is True

def test_unicode_emails():
    """Test unicode emails (should be invalid according to this regex)"""
    assert is_valid_email("üñîçøðé@example.com") is False
    assert is_valid_email("test@exämple.com") is False
    assert is_valid_email("用户@例子.中国") is False

def test_ip_address_emails():
    """Test email addresses with IP addresses as domains"""
    # Valid IP address format
    assert is_valid_email("user@123.123.123.123") is True
    # Invalid IP address formats
    assert is_valid_email("
